note: Body values: markdown passes raw Markdown and does not allow nested tags; htmdx allows Markdown, HTML, and nested registered component tags; none allows no body. Every component accepts class, id, aria-*, and data-* attributes; all other props must be declared. Declared string values stay strings, number values must be finite numbers, boolean values use a bare attribute or true/false, and JSON values must parse as valid JSON before declared constraints run. HTMDX is declarative: imports, exports, brace expressions, event handlers, and function-valued props are not allowed.

components.name: Audience
components.purpose: Show target segments from one or more `- item` rows. Format each item as `**Persona — Type:** Description · metrics: value — caption; … · priority: High|Medium|Unmeasured`; Type is Primary, Secondary, or Secondary · Risk.
components.example:
<Audience>
- **Self-Creator · Custom Goods Merchant — Primary:** Sells made-to-order products and needs a file from the shopper at purchase. · metrics: ~19.8K — stores above $50K GPV; 4.85% — of ~408K active stores · priority: High
- **Partner / Freelancer — Secondary:** Hits the upload gap on every relevant client build. · priority: Medium
</Audience>

components.name: Callout
components.purpose: Emphasize an important note, warning, or takeaway using a Markdown body.

components.name: ChartArea
components.purpose: Compare non-negative numeric values written as '- label: number' rows; currently rendered with the shared bar-chart visualization.

components.name: ChartBar
components.purpose: Compare non-negative numeric values with a bar chart using '- label: number' rows.

components.name: ChartLine
components.purpose: Compare non-negative numeric values written as '- label: number' rows; currently rendered with the shared bar-chart visualization.

components.name: ChartPie
components.purpose: Compare non-negative numeric values written as '- label: number' rows; currently rendered with the shared bar-chart visualization.

components.name: Compare
components.purpose: Place alternatives or contrasting concepts side by side. Write one or more `- item` rows; use `**Title:** details` to label a card.

components.name: DataTable
components.purpose: Display structured records written as a GFM table with a non-empty header, separator row, and at least one consistently sized data row.

components.name: DecisionMatrix
components.purpose: Compare options as GFM-table columns against criteria as rows. Put a ✓ in the chosen option header to highlight it. A body cell may start with `[blue]`, `[green]`, `[amber]`, `[red]`, `[gray]`, or `[purple]` to show a colored status dot.
components.example:
<DecisionMatrix>
| Criterion | A — Text workaround | B — Native upload ✓ | C — Partner app |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| User fit | Partial | [green] Fully solves the intent | Partial |
| Build effort | [green] Low | [amber] Medium | [red] High |
</DecisionMatrix>

components.name: DecisionTable
components.purpose: Present decision criteria and outcomes written as '- label: value' rows, splitting each row at its first colon.
components.example:
<DecisionTable>
- Scope: Built-in components only
- Delivery: Versioned manifest
</DecisionTable>

components.name: Evidence
components.purpose: List evidence that supports the surrounding analysis. Write one or more `- item` rows; use `**Title:** details` to label an evidence card.
components.example:
<Evidence>
- **Runtime:** The allowlist currently lives in implementation code.
</Evidence>

components.name: ExecutiveSummary
components.purpose: Summarize the artifact’s most important conclusion or recommendation.
components.example:
<ExecutiveSummary>
Ship **one HTML file** with editable HTMDX source.
</ExecutiveSummary>

components.name: Finding
components.purpose: Present key findings as a scannable card list. Write one or more `- item` rows; use `**Title:** details` to label a finding.

components.name: IntentList
components.purpose: Group user intents by priority (Blocker/Critical/Nice to have) with a persona · type header, the intent quote, and current → expected feeling chips (red → green). Each item title is `#id · Priority · Persona · Type`; the text is the quote followed by `negative, negative → positive, positive`. Append an optional `· note: …` to flag an intent with a caution strip (e.g. a guardrail) inside its card.
components.example:
<IntentList>
- **#int-001 · Blocker · Self-Creator · Main intent:** "I want to collect a shopper's file at purchase, so the attachment is tied to the order." — frustrated, resigned → in control, relieved
- **#int-001 · Critical · Shopper · Main intent:** "I want to attach my file while buying, so the seller gets exactly what I mean." — uncertain, friction → understood, reassured · note: Required-upload step is an unmeasured conversion risk — guardrail KPI required at launch
</IntentList>

components.name: MetricStrip
components.purpose: Show a compact set of labeled headline values written as '- label: value' rows. A leading trend glyph (↑ up/green, ↓ down/red, ⊘ guardrail/amber) is colored automatically; an ` — ` or ` · ` in the value renders the remainder as a muted caption.
components.example:
<MetricStrip>
- ↑ Active stores using upload: **baseline 0** — post-launch adopter cohort
- ↓ File-upload support tickets: **55** · 3 months post-launch
- ⊘ Buyer checkout-completion rate: **guardrail** — hold at or above baseline
</MetricStrip>

components.name: OpenQuestions
components.purpose: An amber-framed panel of open questions and assumptions, each row carrying a labeled badge. Item title is the label (Assumption = blue, Risk = red, Open = amber); the text is the note.
components.example:
<OpenQuestions>
- **Assumption:** FILE_UPLOAD render type on the Catalog V3 Modifier mechanism. To be confirmed in spec.
- **Risk:** Required-before-add-to-cart carries an unmeasured buyer-conversion risk. Guardrail KPI at launch.
- **Open:** Order-carriage path and buyer-file retention policy are deferred to spec.
</OpenQuestions>

components.name: RiskTable
components.purpose: Classify priorities written as Markdown list rows. Each row must start with one unique, case-sensitive bold tier—`Must-have`, `Differentiator`, `Not now`, or `Won't do`—followed by non-empty text.
components.example:
<RiskTable>
- **Must-have:** Publish exact-version metadata.
- **Not now:** Describe host components.
</RiskTable>

components.name: SignalGrid
components.purpose: A grid of category cards with a colored top accent — for Why-Now signals or a Problem / Root cause / Opportunity trio. Item title is `Category | tone` (tone: blue/green/amber/red/gray/purple); the text is an optional `Headline — ` followed by the body.
components.example:
<SignalGrid>
- **User pain · Escalated | red:** Paying for a workaround — merchants pay $14–60/mo and escalate to support.
- **Competitive gap | amber:** 4 of 6 native competitors ship upload — Wix does not.
</SignalGrid>

components.name: SourceQuote
components.purpose: Display a source quotation or excerpt as Markdown, with attribution supplied in the surrounding content.

components.name: Sources
components.purpose: Show research artifacts as provenance pills prefixed with ↗. Write one or more `- source` rows; make an item bold to highlight the current artifact.

components.name: Stat
components.purpose: Highlight one or more labeled statistics written as '- label: value' rows.

components.name: Timeline
components.purpose: Describe milestones as labeled points in time written as '- label: value' rows, splitting each row at its first colon.
components.example:
<Timeline>
- July: Publish the manifest
- August: Adopt it in validators
</Timeline>

components.name: Accordion
components.purpose: Group of expandable AccordionItem sections; choose single or multiple open items.
components.example:
<Accordion type="single" collapsible defaultValue='"risks"'>
  <AccordionItem value="risks">
    <AccordionTrigger>Key risks</AccordionTrigger>
    <AccordionContent>Vendor lock-in.</AccordionContent>
  </AccordionItem>
</Accordion>

props.name: type
props.description: Whether one or many items can be open at once.

props.name: collapsible
props.description: For type single, lets the open item close when selected again.

props.name: defaultValue
props.description: Initially open item value: a JSON string for type single or a JSON string array for type multiple.

components.name: AccordionContent
components.purpose: Content revealed when its AccordionItem is open.

components.name: AccordionItem
components.purpose: One named expandable section inside an Accordion.

props.name: value
props.description: Unique value that identifies this item within its Accordion.

components.name: AccordionTrigger
components.purpose: Heading control that opens or closes its AccordionItem.

components.name: Alert
components.purpose: Callout banner for a titled note or warning.

props.name: variant
props.description: Visual emphasis of the alert.

components.name: AlertDescription
components.purpose: Supporting body text inside an Alert.

components.name: AlertTitle
components.purpose: Title line inside an Alert.

components.name: AspectRatio
components.purpose: Keeps nested media at a fixed width-to-height ratio.

props.name: ratio
props.description: Width divided by height; use a positive number such as 1.7778 for 16:9.

components.name: Avatar
components.purpose: Round image container that holds an AvatarImage and AvatarFallback.

components.name: AvatarFallback
components.purpose: Initials or short text shown when an AvatarImage is unavailable.

components.name: AvatarImage
components.purpose: Image inside an Avatar; pair it with AvatarFallback for missing images.

props.name: src
props.description: URL of the avatar image.

props.name: alt
props.description: Text alternative for the image.

components.name: Badge
components.purpose: Small status or label chip.

props.name: variant
props.description: Visual emphasis of the chip.

components.name: Breadcrumb
components.purpose: Navigation trail that shows the current page within a hierarchy.

components.name: BreadcrumbEllipsis
components.purpose: Collapsed-items marker inside a BreadcrumbItem.

components.name: BreadcrumbItem
components.purpose: One linked or current-page entry inside a BreadcrumbList.

components.name: BreadcrumbLink
components.purpose: Link to an ancestor page inside a BreadcrumbItem.

props.name: href
props.description: URL of the ancestor page.

components.name: BreadcrumbList
components.purpose: Ordered list that lays out the items and separators in a Breadcrumb.

components.name: BreadcrumbPage
components.purpose: Current, non-clickable page inside a BreadcrumbItem.

components.name: BreadcrumbSeparator
components.purpose: Separator between two BreadcrumbItem elements; uses a chevron when empty.

components.name: Button
components.purpose: Button-styled label for display-only artifact actions.

props.name: asChild
props.description: Use the single child element as the button-styled control.

props.name: variant
props.description: Visual emphasis of the button.

props.name: size
props.description: Button height and padding preset.

components.name: Card
components.purpose: Container for a self-contained block with optional header, content, and footer areas.
components.example:
<Card>
  <CardHeader>
    <CardTitle>Revenue</CardTitle>
    <CardDescription>Quarterly numbers</CardDescription>
  </CardHeader>
  <CardContent>Grew **12%** quarter over quarter.</CardContent>
  <CardFooter><Button variant="outline" size="sm">Details</Button></CardFooter>
</Card>

components.name: CardAction
components.purpose: Action area aligned at the end of a CardHeader.

components.name: CardContent
components.purpose: Main body of a Card; accepts Markdown, HTML, and nested HTMDX components.

components.name: CardDescription
components.purpose: Supporting description line inside a CardHeader.

components.name: CardFooter
components.purpose: Footer row of a Card, usually holding actions or status labels.

components.name: CardHeader
components.purpose: Header area of a Card; holds its title, description, and optional action.

components.name: CardTitle
components.purpose: Title line inside a CardHeader.

components.name: Collapsible
components.purpose: Single expandable region controlled by a CollapsibleTrigger.

props.name: defaultOpen
props.description: Whether the region starts open.

components.name: CollapsibleContent
components.purpose: Content shown while its Collapsible region is open.

components.name: CollapsibleTrigger
components.purpose: Control that opens or closes the related CollapsibleContent.

props.name: asChild
props.description: Use the single child element as the trigger instead of rendering a button.

components.name: Dialog
components.purpose: Modal state container for a trigger and accessible dialog content.
components.example:
<Dialog>
  <DialogTrigger>Open dialog</DialogTrigger>
  <DialogContent>
    <DialogHeader>
      <DialogTitle>Confirm migration</DialogTitle>
      <DialogDescription>Review the migration before continuing.</DialogDescription>
    </DialogHeader>
    <DialogFooter><DialogClose>Cancel</DialogClose></DialogFooter>
  </DialogContent>
</Dialog>

props.name: defaultOpen
props.description: Whether the dialog starts open.

props.name: modal
props.description: Whether the dialog blocks interaction with content outside it.

components.name: DialogClose
components.purpose: Control inside DialogContent that closes the dialog.
components.example:
<Dialog defaultOpen>
  <DialogContent>
    <DialogHeader>
      <DialogTitle>Discard draft?</DialogTitle>
      <DialogDescription>Your unsaved changes will be lost.</DialogDescription>
    </DialogHeader>
    <DialogFooter><DialogClose>Keep editing</DialogClose></DialogFooter>
  </DialogContent>
</Dialog>

props.name: asChild
props.description: Use the single child element as the close control instead of rendering a button.

components.name: DialogContent
components.purpose: Portalled modal surface; include a DialogTitle and DialogDescription for accessibility.
components.example:
<Dialog defaultOpen>
  <DialogContent showCloseButton="true">
    <DialogHeader>
      <DialogTitle>Publish report?</DialogTitle>
      <DialogDescription>The report will become visible to the team.</DialogDescription>
    </DialogHeader>
    <DialogFooter><DialogClose>Cancel</DialogClose></DialogFooter>
  </DialogContent>
</Dialog>

props.name: showCloseButton
props.description: Whether to show the icon close control in the top corner.

props.name: forceMount
props.description: Keep the content mounted even when the dialog is closed.

components.name: DialogDescription
components.purpose: Accessible description that explains a DialogContent surface.
components.example:
<Dialog defaultOpen>
  <DialogContent>
    <DialogHeader>
      <DialogTitle>Archive report?</DialogTitle>
      <DialogDescription>The report will move to the archive.</DialogDescription>
    </DialogHeader>
  </DialogContent>
</Dialog>

components.name: DialogFooter
components.purpose: Bottom action section of DialogContent with an optional built-in close button.
components.example:
<Dialog defaultOpen>
  <DialogContent>
    <DialogHeader>
      <DialogTitle>Save changes?</DialogTitle>
      <DialogDescription>Confirm whether to keep your edits.</DialogDescription>
    </DialogHeader>
    <DialogFooter showCloseButton="true"><Button>Save</Button></DialogFooter>
  </DialogContent>
</Dialog>

props.name: showCloseButton
props.description: Whether to append an outline close button.

components.name: DialogHeader
components.purpose: Top section of DialogContent that groups its accessible title and description.
components.example:
<Dialog defaultOpen>
  <DialogContent>
    <DialogHeader>
      <DialogTitle>Export data?</DialogTitle>
      <DialogDescription>The export may take several minutes.</DialogDescription>
    </DialogHeader>
  </DialogContent>
</Dialog>

components.name: DialogOverlay
components.purpose: Bodyless backdrop layer for a custom DialogPortal; DialogContent already adds one.
components.example:
<Dialog defaultOpen>
  <DialogPortal><DialogOverlay /></DialogPortal>
  <DialogContent>
    <DialogHeader>
      <DialogTitle>Custom backdrop</DialogTitle>
      <DialogDescription>This dialog also demonstrates its overlay layer.</DialogDescription>
    </DialogHeader>
  </DialogContent>
</Dialog>

props.name: forceMount
props.description: Keep the overlay mounted even when the dialog is closed.

components.name: DialogPortal
components.purpose: Moves custom Dialog layers to the document body; DialogContent already uses one.
components.example:
<Dialog defaultOpen>
  <DialogPortal><DialogOverlay /></DialogPortal>
  <DialogContent>
    <DialogHeader>
      <DialogTitle>Portalled dialog</DialogTitle>
      <DialogDescription>The modal layers render outside the source host.</DialogDescription>
    </DialogHeader>
  </DialogContent>
</Dialog>

props.name: forceMount
props.description: Keep the portal children mounted even when the dialog is closed.

components.name: DialogTitle
components.purpose: Required accessible name for a DialogContent surface.
components.example:
<Dialog defaultOpen>
  <DialogContent>
    <DialogHeader>
      <DialogTitle>Delete workspace?</DialogTitle>
      <DialogDescription>This action cannot be undone.</DialogDescription>
    </DialogHeader>
  </DialogContent>
</Dialog>

components.name: DialogTrigger
components.purpose: Control inside Dialog that opens its dialog content.
components.example:
<Dialog>
  <DialogTrigger>Open migration review</DialogTrigger>
  <DialogContent>
    <DialogHeader>
      <DialogTitle>Migration review</DialogTitle>
      <DialogDescription>Check the planned changes.</DialogDescription>
    </DialogHeader>
  </DialogContent>
</Dialog>

props.name: asChild
props.description: Use the single child element as the trigger instead of rendering a button.

components.name: HoverCard
components.purpose: State container for a trigger and a rich preview card shown after a pointer pause.

props.name: defaultOpen
props.description: Whether the card starts open.

props.name: openDelay
props.description: Milliseconds to wait before opening.

props.name: closeDelay
props.description: Milliseconds to wait before closing.

components.name: HoverCardContent
components.purpose: Portalled rich preview surface positioned relative to its HoverCardTrigger.

props.name: forceMount
props.description: Keep the content mounted when closed.

props.name: side
props.description: Preferred side of the trigger.

props.name: sideOffset
props.description: Distance in pixels from the trigger.

props.name: align
props.description: Content alignment along the trigger.

props.name: alignOffset
props.description: Alignment shift in pixels.

props.name: avoidCollisions
props.description: Shift or flip content to keep it in view.

props.name: collisionPadding
props.description: Viewport edge padding in pixels.

props.name: sticky
props.description: How content stays aligned when the trigger leaves view.

props.name: hideWhenDetached
props.description: Hide content when its trigger is fully clipped.

components.name: HoverCardTrigger
components.purpose: Control inside HoverCard whose pointer or focus state reveals HoverCardContent.

props.name: asChild
props.description: Use the single child element as the trigger instead of rendering a link.

components.name: Popover
components.purpose: State container for a control and a floating panel shown on demand.

props.name: defaultOpen
props.description: Whether the popover starts open.

props.name: modal
props.description: Whether open content blocks interaction outside it.

components.name: PopoverAnchor
components.purpose: Optional element inside Popover used as the positioning anchor for PopoverContent.

props.name: asChild
props.description: Use the single child element as the anchor instead of rendering a wrapper.

components.name: PopoverContent
components.purpose: Portalled floating panel positioned relative to a PopoverTrigger or PopoverAnchor.
components.example:
<Popover defaultOpen>
  <PopoverTrigger>Open filters</PopoverTrigger>
  <PopoverContent side="bottom" align="start">Choose the **report filters**.</PopoverContent>
</Popover>

props.name: forceMount
props.description: Keep the content mounted when closed.

props.name: side
props.description: Preferred side of the anchor.

props.name: sideOffset
props.description: Distance in pixels from the anchor.

props.name: align
props.description: Content alignment along the anchor.

props.name: alignOffset
props.description: Alignment shift in pixels.

props.name: avoidCollisions
props.description: Shift or flip content to keep it in view.

props.name: collisionPadding
props.description: Viewport edge padding in pixels.

props.name: sticky
props.description: How content stays aligned when the anchor leaves view.

props.name: hideWhenDetached
props.description: Hide content when its anchor is fully clipped.

components.name: PopoverTrigger
components.purpose: Control inside Popover that opens or closes PopoverContent.

props.name: asChild
props.description: Use the single child element as the trigger instead of rendering a button.

components.name: Progress
components.purpose: Displays determinate progress from 0 to 100.

props.name: value
props.description: Completed percentage from 0 to 100.

components.name: Separator
components.purpose: Draws a horizontal or vertical rule between sections.

props.name: orientation
props.description: Direction of the dividing rule.

props.name: decorative
props.description: Whether assistive tools should ignore the separator.

components.name: Table
components.purpose: Responsive data table root; compose a caption, header, body, and optional footer inside it.

components.name: TableBody
components.purpose: Body section of a Table; place data TableRow elements inside it.

components.name: TableCaption
components.purpose: Accessible caption that names or describes its containing Table.

components.name: TableCell
components.purpose: Data cell inside a TableRow; use spans to cover more than one row or column.

props.name: colSpan
props.description: Number of columns this cell spans.

props.name: rowSpan
props.description: Number of rows this cell spans; 0 spans all remaining rows in the section.

props.name: headers
props.description: Space-separated ids of TableHead cells that label this cell.

components.name: TableFooter
components.purpose: Footer section of a Table for totals or summary rows.

components.name: TableHead
components.purpose: Header cell inside a TableRow; use scope to link it to its row, column, or group.

props.name: colSpan
props.description: Number of columns this header cell spans.

props.name: rowSpan
props.description: Number of rows this header cell spans; 0 spans all remaining rows in the section.

props.name: headers
props.description: Space-separated ids of other TableHead cells that label this header cell.

props.name: scope
props.description: Cells this header labels.

props.name: abbr
props.description: Short form of the header label.

components.name: TableHeader
components.purpose: Header section of a Table; place rows of TableHead cells inside it.

components.name: TableRow
components.purpose: One row inside a TableHeader, TableBody, or TableFooter; fill it with matching cell tags.

components.name: Tabs
components.purpose: Tab group that shows the TabsContent matching the selected TabsTrigger.
components.example:
<Tabs defaultValue="summary">
  <TabsList>
    <TabsTrigger value="summary">Summary</TabsTrigger>
    <TabsTrigger value="details">Details</TabsTrigger>
  </TabsList>
  <TabsContent value="summary">Summary panel.</TabsContent>
  <TabsContent value="details">Details panel.</TabsContent>
</Tabs>

props.name: defaultValue
props.description: Value of the tab selected when the group first renders.

components.name: TabsContent
components.purpose: Panel shown when its value matches the selected TabsTrigger.
components.example:
<Tabs defaultValue="summary">
  <TabsList>
    <TabsTrigger value="summary">Summary</TabsTrigger>
    <TabsTrigger value="details">Details</TabsTrigger>
  </TabsList>
  <TabsContent value="summary">Summary with **key metrics**.</TabsContent>
  <TabsContent value="details">Details panel.</TabsContent>
</Tabs>

props.name: value
props.description: Value that links this panel to its TabsTrigger control.

components.name: TabsList
components.purpose: Container for the TabsTrigger controls in a Tabs group.
components.example:
<Tabs defaultValue="summary">
  <TabsList>
    <TabsTrigger value="summary">Summary</TabsTrigger>
    <TabsTrigger value="details">Details</TabsTrigger>
  </TabsList>
  <TabsContent value="summary">Summary panel.</TabsContent>
  <TabsContent value="details">Details panel.</TabsContent>
</Tabs>

components.name: TabsTrigger
components.purpose: Tab control whose value selects the matching TabsContent.
components.example:
<Tabs defaultValue="summary">
  <TabsList>
    <TabsTrigger value="summary">Summary</TabsTrigger>
    <TabsTrigger value="details">Details</TabsTrigger>
  </TabsList>
  <TabsContent value="summary">Summary panel.</TabsContent>
  <TabsContent value="details">Details panel.</TabsContent>
</Tabs>

props.name: value
props.description: Value that links this control to its TabsContent panel.

components.name: Tooltip
components.purpose: State container for a control and a short text label; includes standalone provider wiring.

props.name: defaultOpen
props.description: Whether the tooltip starts open.

props.name: delayDuration
props.description: Milliseconds to wait before opening this tooltip.

props.name: disableHoverableContent
props.description: Close when the pointer leaves the trigger instead of allowing hover over content.

components.name: TooltipContent
components.purpose: Portalled short label positioned relative to its TooltipTrigger.

props.name: forceMount
props.description: Keep the content mounted when closed.

props.name: side
props.description: Preferred side of the trigger.

props.name: sideOffset
props.description: Distance in pixels from the trigger.

props.name: align
props.description: Content alignment along the trigger.

props.name: alignOffset
props.description: Alignment shift in pixels.

props.name: avoidCollisions
props.description: Shift or flip content to keep it in view.

props.name: collisionPadding
props.description: Viewport edge padding in pixels.

props.name: sticky
props.description: How content stays aligned when the trigger leaves view.

props.name: hideWhenDetached
props.description: Hide content when its trigger is fully clipped.

components.name: TooltipProvider
components.purpose: Shared timing and pointer-behavior settings for one or more nested Tooltip components.

props.name: delayDuration
props.description: Milliseconds to wait before opening a tooltip.

props.name: skipDelayDuration
props.description: Grace period for moving between tooltip triggers without another delay.

props.name: disableHoverableContent
props.description: Close when the pointer leaves a trigger instead of allowing hover over content.

components.name: TooltipTrigger
components.purpose: Control inside Tooltip whose pointer or focus state reveals TooltipContent.

props.name: asChild
props.description: Use the single child element as the trigger instead of rendering a button.
